pageTitle.value: An interview with James Cameron - Rolex and Cinema

pageCaption.value: James Cameron is acknowledged for using trail-blazing technology in his films, but when it comes to advice for emerging filmmakers he says to “stay in touch with the human heart”. The director of Titanic and Avatar believes nothing is more important than “speaking with an authentic voice and portraying the human condition at its most elemental level”.

WaPageTitle.value: An interview with James Cameron - Rolex and Cinema

metadescription.value: Acclaimed director James Cameron is responsible for some of the most memorable films of the past three decades. Watch the interview with him now.

previewTitle.value: An interview with James Cameron - Rolex and Cinema

pageTitle.value: An interview with Martin Scorsese - Rolex and Cinema

pageCaption.value: To future filmmakers Martin Scorsese says: “Don’t be afraid to try anything, no matter how crazy it might seem to others.” These are encouraging words from this director, producer and screenwriter who is responsible for many of the biggest classics in film history. He is always pushing boundaries and helping to promote the creative spark in others.

WaPageTitle.value: An interview with Martin Scorsese - Rolex and Cinema

metadescription.value: Martin Scorsese's career has spanned 50 years and as many films. Watch an interview with the director, producer and screenwriter now.

previewTitle.value: An interview with Martin Scorsese - Rolex and Cinema

pageTitle.value: Spike Lee & Kyle Bell - Rolex mentoring programme - Video

pageCaption.value: Spike Lee, one of today’s most socially conscious filmmakers, chose to mentor Kyle Bell, a young filmmaker from the Thlopthlocco Creek Tribal Town of Oklahoma, to help expand the boundaries of his evocative filmmaking.

WaPageTitle.value: Spike Lee and Kyle Bell - Video

metadescription.value: Rolex mentor Spike Lee has helped his protégé Kyle Bell be vocal about his vision and lead other people along the way. Watch the video on rolex.org.

previewTitle.value: Spike Lee & Kyle Bell - Rolex mentoring programme - Video

pageTitle.value: Alfonso Cuarón and Chaitanya Tamhane - Video

pageCaption.value: For Indian protégé Chaitanya Tamhane, his mentoring year with Academy-award winning film director Alfonso Cuarón was a stroke of fortune. He describes his mentor’s image-based film-making as “like watching magic”. Tamhane says his approach to cinema will never be the same again.

pageTitle.value: Alejandro G. Iñárritu and Tom Shoval - Video

pageCaption.value: Young Israeli director Tom Shoval’s year of mentoring evolved with the excitement worthy of a Hollywood film script. Shoval spent weeks on the set of Alejandro G. Iñárritu’s film 'The Revenant' with the Oscar winner revealing all the “infinite possibilities” of film-making.

pageTitle.value: Joan Jonas and Thao Nguyen Phan - Video

pageCaption.value: “Art is a spiritual practice,” says Joan Jonas, pioneer of performance and video art. For her Vietnamese protégée Thao Nguyen Phan, the experience of working with Jonas and observing her constant experimentation has taught her to be more open to what she can use to tell the stories that underlie her work.
